surface_styles.inputs: bg-transparent border-b border-[#E6E2DA] focus:border-[#C75D3B] outline-none rounded-none

header.layout: Flex space-between. Logo on left (h-12 object-contain), Nav links (Showreel, About, Contact) on right.
header.interactions: Hover states on nav links change to accent color (#C75D3B) with smooth transition.

hero.content: Poetic headline (e.g., 'Crafting light and shadows into stories.'). Tagline: 'Paper & Brush Pictures. Since 2017.' CTA Buttons: 'View Films' (solid charcoal), 'Get in Touch' (outline charcoal).
hero.alignment: Left-aligned or centered, depending on the visual balance, but prioritize massive negative space.

featured_film.content: Large embedded YouTube player (ID: WmtGrkCIS-M). Container should be responsive, max-w-6xl mx-auto, aspect-video.
featured_film.style: No heavy borders. Maybe a subtle shadow or just pure flat. Generous breathing room around it.

showreel_grid.layout: Grid cols-1 md:cols-2 lg:cols-3. Gap-8 to gap-12.
showreel_grid.item_structure: Image wrapper (overflow-hidden, aspect-video), Thumbnail image (vumbnail.com), underneath: Film Title (serif, text-xl), subtle category/role label (sans-serif, text-xs uppercase).
showreel_grid.interactions: On hover, the image scales slightly (scale-105) within its wrapper. Click opens Dialog with embedded Vimeo player.

about.layout: 2-column layout. Left: Large poetic studio statement ('We are a boutique studio...'). Right: Disciplines list (Direction, Commercials, Brand Films, Documentary, Digital) and 'Based in Mumbai'.
about.typography: Left side uses massive serif text (text-4xl md:text-5xl). Right side uses structured sans-serif typography.

contact.layout: Grid cols-1 md:cols-2. Left: Contact details (Email, Phone, Address, Socials). Right: 'Send a brief' Form.
contact.form_style: Minimalist fields. Only bottom borders (border-[#5C5A56] focus:border-[#C75D3B]). No background color for inputs. Button should be a pill or sharp rectangle with a white background and black text.

footer.content: Minimal. Copyright text. Large 'Paper & Brush' typographic watermark if space permits.

components_to_use.dialog: Use Shadcn Dialog for the Vimeo Lightbox (ensure to output .js/.jsx, not .tsx). Must be customized to cover most of the screen (max-w-5xl, bg-black).
components_to_use.form: Use Shadcn Form, Input, Textarea. Remove default card look; make them bare, editorial, bottom-border only.
components_to_use.buttons: Use Shadcn Button. Override defaults to be poetic (pill or sharp, use brand colors).

motion_and_interactions.scrolling: Implement Lenis for smooth scrolling to give a premium, cinematic feel.
motion_and_interactions.entrance: Use framer-motion for staggered text reveal on the Hero and About sections (e.g., initial={opacity:0, y:20} animate={opacity:1, y:0}).
motion_and_interactions.hover: All clickable items (nav links, films, buttons) must have subtle, intentional transition durations (duration-300).

instructions_to_main_agent: NOTE: You have .js/.jsx files in this environment, not .tsx. Implement all components (including Shadcn components) using standard JavaScript/JSX syntax without TypeScript annotations.
Add the Google Fonts link in the public index.html.
Use Shadcn CLI to add Dialog, Form, Input, Label, Button. (Adapt to .js if required)
Do NOT use 'Inter' for text. Use Cormorant Garamond for headings and Outfit for body.
For the Film Grid, use 'https://vumbnail.com/${vimeoId}.jpg' as the image source.
When a film thumbnail is clicked, open a Shadcn Dialog. Inside the Dialog content, place a responsive iframe pointing to 'https://player.vimeo.com/video/${vimeoId}?autoplay=1'.
Keep spacing extremely generous (p-12, py-32). Minimalist editorial design breathes through empty space.
NEVER use fully transparent backgrounds for the sticky header; it must have a backdrop-blur and a slight fill.
Add data-testid attributes to all interactive elements (e.g., 'showreel-link', 'contact-form-submit', 'film-card-${id}').
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: Do not converge toward generic 'AI slop' aesthetics. Make the UI distinctive and beautiful.
Create depth with layered elements and deliberate z-index hierarchies.
Do not apply `transition: all` globally; apply specific transitions (e.g., `transition-colors`, `transition-transform`).
Do not center-align everything by default. Left-alignment often works better for readability and poetic tension.
Ensure every interactive element has a visible focus state for accessibility.
Never use generic AI emojis in the UI.
Ensure all images and videos have proper aspect ratios and object-fit properties (e.g., object-cover).
